title: bigelovia umbellata
intro: Bigelovia umbellata, also known as Mountain Pride or Bigelow's Bells, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Bigelovia umbellata is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used in salves and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
